message:
perf(scheduler): track local task locality in IntrusiveStack and add CachePadded counters

IntrusiveStack now maintains a local_count field that tracks how many
!Send (local) tasks are currently in the stack. This enables O(1)
has_local_tasks() queries, allowing callers to skip per-task locality
checks in common steal paths.

Three new fast-path methods exploit this:
- push_assume_non_local(): skips locality bookkeeping when the caller has
  already proven the task is non-local (used in steal-batch hot paths)
- steal_batch_into_non_local(): bulk-steals tasks without per-item local
  checks when the source stack has no local tasks
- steal_one_assume_non_local(): single-task steal without locality
  overhead, returning TaskId directly instead of (TaskId, bool) tuple

The Stealer in local_queue.rs now checks has_local_tasks() before
entering the steal loop. When the source queue contains only stealable
(non-local) tasks — the common case — it dispatches directly to
steal_batch_into_non_local(), bypassing the temporary local-task buffer
and per-task locality branching entirely.

GlobalInjector wraps ready_count and timed_count in CachePadded to
eliminate false sharing between producer and consumer cores polling
different lanes. ThreeLaneWorker's WorkerCoordinator similarly pads
next_wake, and the main scheduler loop downgrades shutdown flag loads
from Acquire to Relaxed since the shutdown flag is only a hint checked
each iteration (actual cleanup is sequenced by the join protocol).

All existing push/pop/push_back/steal operations maintain local_count
with debug_assert! guards on underflow and locality invariants. New
tests cover local_count tracking through mixed push/steal/pop sequences
and ordering preservation in steal_batch_into_non_local.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
